title: Dried Ukrainian Grapes Tagine
description: A salty and pillowy Ukrainian dish featuring dried grapes, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a tagine or large clay pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the diced bell peppers and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the chickpeas, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Add the dried grapes and simmer, uncovered, for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid has thickened and the grapes are rehydrated.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve over couscous or rice.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
